summary: Extend mock classes in selection.test.ts: add `sub(v)`, `multiplyScalar(s)` to MockVector3; add `getWorldDirection(target)` to MockPerspectiveCamera. These are needed for V-02/V-03 tests but should be added upfront to avoid mixing mock infrastructure changes with test logic.; V-10: Write failing tests for click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.test.ts. New describe block 'click-vs-drag discrimination' with tests: (1) pointerdown alone does NOT fire onSelect, (2) pointerdown+pointerup at same position fires onSelect with raycasted entity, (3) pointerdown+pointerup with >5px movement does NOT fire onSelect, (4) pointerdown+pointerup with <=5px movement fires onSelect, (5) onSelect receives null when pointerup raycast misses, (6) after dispose, pointerup does not fire onSelect.; V-10: Implement click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.ts. Replace `handlePointerDown` (which fires onSelect immediately) with: (a) `handlePointerDown` that records {clientX, clientY} and adds a one-shot pointerup listener, (b) `handlePointerUp` that computes Euclidean distance from pointerdown position and only calls raycast()+onSelect() if distance < 5px. Update dispose() to also remove pointerup listener. Update existing 'click-based selection raycasting' tests and 'rAF-throttled pointermove / pointerdown still raycasts synchronously' test to dispatch both pointerdown+pointerup events instead of just pointerdown.; V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for fitToView with view-direction offset and controls.target update. In selection.test.ts, add tests: (1) fitToView offsets camera along current view direction (not just Z) — set camera at an angle, verify position is center - viewDir*distance, (2) fitToView updates controls.target to bounding box center when controls provided via SelectionOptions, (3) fitToView still works without controls (backward compat). Update setup() to optionally accept a controls mock with a target Vector3.; V-02/V-03: Fix fitToView in selection.ts. Add optional `controls?: { target: { copy: (v: any) => void } }` field to SelectionOptions interface. In fitToView(): (a) get view direction via `camera.getWorldDirection(dir)`, (b) position camera at `center - viewDir * distance` instead of just Z offset, (c) if controls provided, set `controls.target.copy(center)`. Update existing fitToView tests that asserted Z-only offset to match new view-direction behavior.; V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for flyToEntity with view-direction offset and controls.target update. Tests: (1) flyToEntity offsets camera along current view direction, (2) flyToEntity updates controls.target to entity bounding box center, (3) flyToEntity with unknown entity does not crash or update controls.target.; V-02/V-03: Fix flyToEntity in selection.ts. Apply same view-direction offset and controls.target update pattern as fitToView. Update existing flyToEntity tests that asserted Z-only offset.

Completed final two steps: step-7 test was already written by prior iteration but uncommitted (confirmed it fails correctly), committed it. step-8 added `controls: controls.controls` to createSelection call in Viewport.tsx to thread OrbitControls through for orbit target updates. All 70 tests pass (56 selection + 14 Viewport).

Write failing test in meshManager.test.ts: when data.normals is null, createMeshFromData calls geometry.computeVertexNormals() (V-04). Add computeVertexNormals mock to MockBufferGeometry. Test: sync with null normals, assert computeVertexNormals was called. Also test: sync with provided normals, assert computeVertexNormals was NOT called.; Implement V-04 in meshManager.ts: In createMeshFromData, add else branch after the normals check — when data.normals is null, call geometry.computeVertexNormals(). In updateMeshGeometry, in the else-if branch where normals are removed (line 97-98), call geometry.computeVertexNormals() after deleteAttribute.; Write failing test in meshManager.test.ts: material is created with side: THREE.DoubleSide (V-05). Add DoubleSide constant to mock. Assert that the material's side property equals DoubleSide (2).; Implement V-05 in meshManager.ts: Import DoubleSide from 'three'. Add side: DoubleSide to the MeshStandardMaterial constructor options.; Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts for mesh data validation (V-06). Tests: (1) sync with vertices.length not divisible by 3 does not add mesh to scene, (2) sync with an index >= vertex count does not add mesh, (3) valid data still works normally. Also test update path: update with invalid data skips the update.; Implement V-06 in meshManager.ts: Add validateMeshData(data: MeshData): boolean that checks vertices.length % 3 === 0 and max(indices) < vertices.length / 3. Guard createMeshFromData and updateMeshGeometry calls in sync() with this validation, logging a console.warn for invalid data.; Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts for safe buffer updates when array length changes (V-07). Tests: (1) update with same-length arrays reuses existing BufferAttribute (identity check — existing test), (2) update with different-length vertex array creates a new BufferAttribute (not same reference), (3) update with different-length index array creates new index BufferAttribute, (4) new BufferAttribute has correct array and count.; Implement V-07 in meshManager.ts: In updateMeshGeometry, before reusing posAttr, check if posAttr.array.length !== data.vertices.length — if so, create new BufferAttribute via setAttribute instead of mutating. Same logic for index and normals.
No new steps completed

Steps 9-15 were already implemented by prior iteration but plan.json was not updated. Verified all tests pass. Step 16: added refreshSelected to createSelection return object, added selection.refreshSelected() call after meshManager.sync() in Viewport.tsx, updated Viewport test mock. Add three-mesh-bvh to gui/package.json dependencies and run npm install. This package provides computeBoundsTree/disposeBoundsTree for BufferGeometry BVH acceleration and acceleratedRaycast for Mesh.prototype.raycast. Required for P-01 (BVH raycasting).; P-03 render-on-demand: Write failing tests in Viewport.test.tsx. Update controls mock to include addEventListener/removeEventListener on the controls object (currently just {}). Add test: 'animate loop does NOT call renderer.render when idle after initial frame' — use capturing rAF mock, fire first rAF callback (initial render), clear renderer.render mock, fire second rAF callback with no interactions, assert renderer.render was NOT called. This test fails against current code which always renders every frame.; P-03 render-on-demand: Implement in Viewport.tsx. Add let needsRender = true flag. Add requestRender() helper that sets needsRender = true. Register controls.controls.addEventListener('change', requestRender) to mark dirty on camera movement. In animate loop: keep calling controls.update() every frame (required for enableDamping), but guard renderer.render(scene, camera) behind if(needsRender), then reset needsRender = false. Add controls.controls.removeEventListener('change', requestRender) in onCleanup. Initial needsRender = true ensures first frame renders.; P-03 render triggers: Write tests verifying all re-render triggers. (1) 'controls change event triggers re-render on next frame' — fire controls mock's captured 'change' callback, then fire rAF, assert renderer.render called. (2) 'resize triggers re-render' — verify renderer.render is called on next frame after ResizeObserver fires. (3) Test that renderer.render is still called once for initial frame (existing test 'animate loop does not call renderer.render after cleanup/dispose' should continue to pass).; P-03 render triggers: Wire requestRender() calls in Viewport.tsx at all trigger points. Add requestRender() after meshManager.sync(props.meshes) in the mesh sync createEffect. Add requestRender() after selection.setHovered() and selection.setSelected() in their createEffects. Add requestRender() after resize() call in the ResizeObserver callback (alongside existing invalidateRect). Verify all tests from step-3 pass plus existing tests remain green.; P-02 EdgesGeometry: Write failing test in selection.test.ts. Add MockEdgesGeometry to the three mock (identical structure to existing MockWireframeGeometry). Add test: 'setSelected creates EdgesGeometry from mesh geometry' — verify that when setSelected('A') is called, EdgesGeometry constructor is invoked with mesh.geometry and the resulting LineSegments is added to scene. Update existing wireframe tests to use EdgesGeometry mock if needed. This test fails because current code imports/uses WireframeGeometry.; P-02 EdgesGeometry: In selection.ts, change import from 'WireframeGeometry' to 'EdgesGeometry' in the three import statement. In setSelected(), replace 'new WireframeGeometry(mesh.geometry)' with 'new EdgesGeometry(mesh.geometry)'. EdgesGeometry is a direct drop-in replacement that produces LineSegments-compatible geometry but only includes edges where adjacent face normals differ by > 1 degree threshold, making it dramatically lighter for dense meshes while preserving the visual outline.; P-01 BVH geometry: Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts. Add computeBoundsTree = vi.fn() and disposeBoundsTree = vi.fn() to MockBufferGeometry. Mock the 'three-mesh-bvh' module to export computeBoundsTree, disposeBoundsTree, acceleratedRaycast. Add tests: (1) 'sync calls geometry.computeBoundsTree() on newly created mesh', (2) 'sync calls geometry.computeBoundsTree() after updating existing mesh geometry', (3) 'removeMesh calls geometry.disposeBoundsTree() before disposing geometry'. These fail because current meshManager doesn't invoke BVH methods.; P-01 BVH geometry: In meshManager.ts, import { computeBoundsTree, disposeBoundsTree } from 'three-mesh-bvh' and BufferGeometry from 'three'. Apply prototype patches at module scope: BufferGeometry.prototype.computeBoundsTree = computeBoundsTree; BufferGeometry.prototype.disposeBoundsTree = disposeBoundsTree. In createMeshFromData(), call geometry.computeBoundsTree() after all attributes are set. In updateMeshGeometry(), call geometry.computeBoundsTree() after updating all attributes (rebuilds BVH for changed geometry). In removeMesh(), call (mesh.geometry as any).disposeBoundsTree() before geometry.dispose().; P-01 BVH raycasting: Write failing test in selection.test.ts. Add mock for 'three-mesh-bvh' module. Add test: 'raycaster has firstHitOnly set to true' — after createSelection, inspect the mock Raycaster instance to verify firstHitOnly property is true. This test fails because current code doesn't set firstHitOnly on the raycaster.

Completed final 3 steps. step-10: Implemented BVH-accelerated raycasting in selection.ts by importing acceleratedRaycast from three-mesh-bvh, patching Mesh.prototype.raycast, and setting firstHitOnly=true on raycaster. step-11: Added edge case test for sync({}) removing all meshes. step-12: Replaced Set allocation with 'in' operator for O(1) key lookup without allocation overhead. All 106 tests pass (58 selection + 17 meshManager + 19 Viewport + 5 controls + 7 scene).

P-01 BVH raycasting: In selection.ts, import { acceleratedRaycast } from 'three-mesh-bvh' and import Mesh from 'three'. Apply prototype patch at module scope: Mesh.prototype.raycast = acceleratedRaycast. After creating the Raycaster instance (line 42), set (raycaster as any).firstHitOnly = true. This enables BVH-accelerated raycasting that exits early on first hit, which is correct since we only use intersections[0].object.name.; P-04 sync optimization: Run existing meshManager tests to verify they all pass (behavior is unchanged, this is a pure refactor). Optionally add test: 'sync with empty incoming object removes all existing meshes' to ensure edge case coverage if not already covered.; P-04 sync optimization: Refactor sync() in meshManager.ts. Replace 'const incomingKeys = new Set(Object.keys(meshes))' and 'incomingKeys.has(key)' with direct property check: 'if (!(key in meshes))'. This avoids one Set + Array allocation per sync call while maintaining identical O(1) lookup semantics. Iterate meshMap.keys() for removal check, then Object.entries(meshes) for add/update (same as current).
Fixed resource leak in resize test by wrapping test body in try/finally so globalThis.ResizeObserver is always restored even if assertions throw. All 19 Viewport tests pass. All 13 steps complete.

Write failing test: E-05 — diagnostics from non-active URI should not be applied to editor. In Editor.test.tsx, add a test in a new 'Editor diagnostics URI filtering' describe block. Simulate a Tauri diagnostics event by capturing the listen handler from the mock, then invoke it with a payload whose `uri` does NOT match the active file's URI. Assert that the editor's diagnostics remain empty (no `cm-diagnostic` elements in DOM). Also add a positive test: diagnostics whose URI matches the active file ARE applied.; Fix E-05 — filter diagnostics by URI. In the `createDiagnosticsListener` callback in Editor.tsx (around line 144), add a guard: `if (event.uri !== currentUri) return;` before converting and applying diagnostics. This ensures only diagnostics for the currently active file are applied to the editor.; Write failing test: RC-04 — debounced didChange should not fire after file switch. In Editor.test.tsx, add a test that: (1) opens file1 in editor, (2) dispatches an edit to the CM6 view (triggering debounce timers), (3) immediately switches to file2 via store.setActiveFile (before 300ms elapses), (4) advances timers past 300ms with vi.advanceTimersByTime(300), (5) asserts that bridge.updateSource was NOT called for the stale edit (timer should have been cancelled by the file switch).; Fix RC-04 — clear debounce timers on file switch. In the `createEffect` for active file changes in Editor.tsx (around line 168), add `clearTimeout(debounceTimer); clearTimeout(lspDebounceTimer);` right after the early return check and before performing the document swap. This cancels any pending debounced operations from the previous file.; Write failing test: E-04 — file switch should preserve per-file undo history (EditorState swap). In Editor.test.tsx, add a test that: (1) opens file1, (2) makes an edit to file1 (insert '// edit\n' at position 0), (3) confirms edit is in the doc, (4) switches to file2, (5) confirms file2 content is shown, (6) switches back to file1, (7) confirms file1 content includes the edit, (8) dispatches undo command, (9) asserts file1 content reverts to original — proving undo history is per-file. A second test verifies that undoing in file2 after switching does NOT produce file1's content.; Fix E-04 — swap per-file EditorState instead of replacing doc content. Add a `Map<string, EditorState>` (fileStates) to Editor.tsx. Extract the CM6 extensions array into a `const extensions = [...]` variable before `EditorState.create()`. In the file switch effect: (1) save `view.state` into `fileStates.set(previousActiveFile, view.state)`, (2) check if `fileStates.has(activeFile)` — if yes, `view.setState(fileStates.get(activeFile)!)`, otherwise create a fresh `EditorState.create({doc: newContent, extensions})` and use `view.setState()`. Remove the old `view.dispatch({changes: ...})` approach and the `isSwitching` flag (no longer needed since setState doesn't trigger updateListener).

Completed all remaining steps. step-7: Fixed buggy RC-02 test (was calling .startsWith on objects instead of .method property), confirmed it fails correctly showing unserialized LSP calls. step-8: Added fileOpsPromise chain to serialize LSP didClose/didOpen across rapid file switches, captured lspVersion by value. step-9: Added integration test combining all four fixes (E-05 diagnostics filtering, RC-04 debounce cancellation, E-04 per-file undo, RC-02 LSP serialization). step-10: Added fileStates.clear() in onCleanup, confirmed isSwitching already removed, all 513 GUI tests pass.

Write failing test: RC-02 — rapid file switches should serialize LSP didClose/didOpen. In Editor.test.tsx, add a test with 3 files (file1, file2, file3). Mock lspClient methods to track call order. Switch file1->file2 then immediately file2->file3 (two setActiveFile calls in sequence). Flush all promises. Assert the LSP call order is exactly: didClose(uri1), didOpen(uri2), didClose(uri2), didOpen(uri3) — proving serialization (each pair completes before the next begins).; Fix RC-02 — serialize LSP file operations with a promise chain. Add `let fileOpsPromise: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve()` to Editor.tsx. In the file switch effect, replace the fire-and-forget promise chain with: `fileOpsPromise = fileOpsPromise.then(() => lspClient.didClose(oldUri)).then(() => lspClient.didOpen(newUri, newContent, lspVersion)).catch(err => console.error('LSP file switch error:', err))`. This ensures each file switch's LSP calls complete before the next pair starts.; Write integration-level test: rapid file switch with diagnostics arriving mid-switch. In Editor.test.tsx, combine all four fixes: (1) open files A and B, (2) edit file A, (3) switch A->B, (4) fire a diagnostics event for file A's URI, (5) advance timers past debounce period, (6) verify: diagnostics NOT applied to B's editor, debounced updateSource NOT called, LSP calls correctly ordered. This serves as a regression test for the combined behavior.; Final cleanup: ensure onCleanup handler also clears the fileStates map. Remove the `isSwitching` flag and guard if it's no longer needed (setState doesn't trigger updateListener). Run the full gui test suite to confirm no regressions across all test files.

Step 11: Added failing test for RC-05 cleanup race condition. Uses deferred promise pattern to control timing: resolve didClose(file1) then immediately unmount while didOpen(file2) is still pending. Asserts no phantom didOpen fires after cleanup. Test correctly fails showing the bug (1 phantom didOpen call). Step 12: Added destroyed flag and chained cleanup didClose off fileOpsPromise. Guard didOpen in file switch chain with if(destroyed) check. Cleanup sets destroyed=true then chains didClose off fileOpsPromise. All 515 GUI tests pass, all 25 Editor tests pass.

Install @codemirror/search package: add to gui/package.json dependencies and run npm install to generate lockfile entry. This is needed for E-02 find/replace support.; E-01 test: Add test in Editor.test.tsx in a new 'Editor extensions' describe block asserting that the rendered editor contains a .cm-lineNumbers element (CodeMirror renders a gutter with this class when lineNumbers() extension is loaded).; E-01 impl: In Editor.tsx, import { lineNumbers } from '@codemirror/view' (add to existing import on line 3) and add lineNumbers() to the extensions array before bracketMatching().; E-02 test: Add test in Editor.test.tsx asserting that after dispatching a Ctrl+F keydown event on the editor's contentDOM, a .cm-search element appears in the editor container (CodeMirror's search panel).; E-02 impl: In Editor.tsx, import { search } from '@codemirror/search' and import { searchKeymap } from '@codemirror/search'. Add search() to the extensions array and add ...searchKeymap to the keymap.of() array.; E-03 test: Add test in Editor.test.tsx. Set up editor with an empty document, type '{' by dispatching view.dispatch({changes: {from: 0, insert: '{'}}). This alone won't trigger closeBrackets (it operates via keyboard input handling). Instead, verify the extension is loaded by checking that the closeBrackets extension is present: use a simulated input event or check that the extension configuration includes closeBrackets. Alternatively, verify via the editor state's facet that closeBrackets is active. Simplest approach: fire a keyboard event for '{' key and check that doc contains '{}'.; E-03 impl: In Editor.tsx, import { closeBrackets, closeBracketsKeymap } from '@codemirror/autocomplete' (extend existing import on line 6). Add closeBrackets() to the extensions array after bracketMatching(). Add ...closeBracketsKeymap to the keymap.of() array before ...defaultKeymap.

Steps 7-8 (E-11 completion from-position) were already implemented in prior iteration commit 989fb0b but plan not updated — marked done. Step 9: Added failing tests for cross-file goto-definition onNavigate callback in gotoDefinition.test.ts. Step 10: Added optional onNavigate parameter to reifyGotoDefinition — when definition URI differs from current file, calls onNavigate(uri, line, character) instead of silently skipping. Step 11: Added failing test for hover tooltip CSS — verifies cm-lsp-hover class and no inline styles. Step 12: Added .cm-lsp-hover CSS rules in Editor.module.css and removed inline styles from hover.ts create(). All 555 GUI tests pass.

E-11 test: Add test in completions.test.ts: 'completion result.from scans backward to word start'. Create a context mock where pos=3, doc text is 'wid' (line from=0, to=3), and doc.sliceString(from, to) returns the text between the given offsets. Call the completion source and assert result.from === 0 (start of 'wid'), not 3 (cursor pos). Add second test with cursor mid-word after non-word chars: text='x = wid', pos=7, line.from=0 — expect from=4 (start of 'wid' not 'x').; E-11 impl: In completions.ts, before the return statement (line 112), compute the word start by scanning backward from pos. Get the line text via state.doc.sliceString(line.from, pos), iterate backward to find the first non-identifier char (not matching /[a-zA-Z0-9_]/), and set `const wordStart = line.from + i + 1` where i is the index of the last non-identifier char. Use wordStart as `from` in the returned CompletionResult instead of `pos`.; E-12 test: Add tests in gotoDefinition.test.ts. Test 1: 'calls onNavigate callback when definition is in a different file'. Create extension with onNavigate spy, simulate Ctrl+Click, mock invoke to return a location with a different URI. Assert onNavigate was called with (differentUri, line, character) and view.dispatch was NOT called. Test 2: 'same-file definition still dispatches cursor movement without calling onNavigate'. Same setup but location URI matches current URI. Assert view.dispatch was called and onNavigate was NOT called.; E-12 impl: Modify reifyGotoDefinition signature to accept optional onNavigate callback: reifyGotoDefinition(uri, onNavigate?: (targetUri: string, line: number, character: number) => void). In the mousedown handler, when location.uri differs from current URI: if onNavigate is provided, call onNavigate(location.uri, location.range.start.line, location.range.start.character); otherwise silently skip (preserving backward compatibility). In Editor.tsx, pass an onNavigate callback that: (a) extracts path from URI by stripping 'file://' prefix, (b) calls bridge.openFile(path) to load content, (c) calls props.store.openFile({path, content}) to open in store, (d) defers cursor navigation by dispatching to the view after the file switch effect runs (use setTimeout or Promise.resolve to yield to the reactive update).; E-14 test: Add test in hover.test.ts: 'tooltip create() returns DOM with cm-lsp-hover class and verifiable structure'. Call the hover handler to get a tooltip result, invoke the create() function on the returned tooltip, and assert that dom.className includes 'cm-lsp-hover'. This validates the CSS selector will match.; E-14 impl: In Editor.module.css, add :global(.cm-lsp-hover) CSS rule with background-color (dark theme: #2d2d3f or var(--reify-tooltip-bg)), color (#e0e0e0), border (1px solid #444), border-radius (4px), box-shadow (0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)), padding (6px 10px), max-width (480px), font-size (13px), white-space (pre-wrap), and line-height (1.4). Remove the redundant inline styles from hover.ts create() (padding, maxWidth, whiteSpace) since CSS now handles them — keep only className assignment and textContent.

Step 13: Added failing integration test in Editor.test.tsx verifying cross-file goto-definition wires through Editor.tsx — Ctrl+Click with cross-file definition response should call bridge.openFile and switch store activeFile. Test correctly failed because Editor.tsx passed no onNavigate callback. Step 14: Wired onNavigate callback in Editor.tsx — imports bridge.openFile as bridgeOpenFile, passes callback to reifyGotoDefinition that strips file:// prefix, loads file via bridge, opens in store, and defers cursor navigation with setTimeout + bounds checking. All 556 GUI tests pass. All 14 plan steps complete.

E-12 integration test (REVIEW FIX): Add test in Editor.test.tsx in a new 'Editor cross-file goto-definition (E-12)' describe block. The test verifies that when a Ctrl+Click triggers a goto-definition response pointing to a different file, Editor.tsx actually navigates to that file via bridge.openFile and store.openFile. Setup: (1) render Editor with file1 active, (2) mock invoke to return a cross-file definition location (uri: 'file:///project/src/mount.ri', range.start: {line: 5, character: 2}) when textDocument/definition is requested, (3) spy on bridge.openFile to return file2's FileData, (4) get the EditorView and simulate Ctrl+Click via a MouseEvent on contentDOM with ctrlKey:true (use view.posAtCoords mock or directly dispatch mousedown on contentDOM). After awaiting the async chain (vi.waitFor), assert: (a) bridge.openFile was called with '/project/src/mount.ri' (the path extracted from the URI), (b) store.state.activeFile === file2.path (the store switched to the target file). This test will FAIL because Editor.tsx:73 currently passes no onNavigate callback.; E-12 integration impl (REVIEW FIX): Wire the onNavigate callback in Editor.tsx. (1) Add `openFile as bridgeOpenFile` to the import from '../bridge' (line 10, alongside updateSource and saveFile). (2) At line 73, change `reifyGotoDefinition(() => currentUri)` to `reifyGotoDefinition(() => currentUri, (targetUri, line, character) => { ... })`. (3) The callback body: (a) strip 'file://' prefix from targetUri to get the filesystem path — `const path = targetUri.replace('file://', '')`, (b) call `bridgeOpenFile(path).then(fileData => { ... })` to load the file from disk, (c) inside the .then: call `props.store.openFile(fileData)` to add/switch to the file in the store (this sets activeFile, which triggers the createEffect file-switch), (d) use `setTimeout(() => { if (view) { const targetLine = view.state.doc.line(line + 1); const targetPos = targetLine.from + character; view.dispatch({ selection: { anchor: targetPos }, scrollIntoView: true }); } }, 0)` to defer cursor navigation until after SolidJS's reactive file-switch effect has run and the EditorView has the new document. (4) Add `.catch((err: unknown) => console.error('Cross-file goto-definition error:', err))` for error handling on the bridgeOpenFile promise.

Step 3: Verified all 43 sidecar tests pass (including 3 entrypoint wiring tests) before S3 refactor. Step 4: Created gui/sidecar/src/cli.ts as dedicated entrypoint that imports+calls main(). Removed fragile auto-run detection (process.argv[1].endsWith) from index.ts. Updated package.json build script to compile both entrypoints. All 43 tests pass, typecheck clean, build produces both cli.js and index.js.

S3: Verify existing entrypoint tests still pass (they import main from index.js and test it directly). No new test file needed — the existing 'entrypoint wiring' tests in session.test.ts fully cover main() behavior. This step is a verification checkpoint before the structural refactor.; S3: Create gui/sidecar/src/cli.ts — a minimal entrypoint that imports main from './index.js' and calls `main().catch((err: unknown) => { console.error('Sidecar fatal error:', err); process.exit(1); })`. Remove the auto-run detection block (lines 60-71: isDirectRun variable and if block) from index.ts. Update gui/sidecar/package.json build script from `tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts` to `tsup src/index.ts src/cli.ts --format esm --dts` so both library and CLI entrypoint are built.
Debug fix for: Failures: tests failed

Create gui/src/panels/chat/ directory and index.ts barrel export file; Create gui/src/panels/chat/markdown.ts — lightweight markdown-to-HTML renderer supporting code blocks (``` fenced), inline code (`), bold (**), italic (*), headers (#-###), links ([text](url)), and unordered lists (- item). Uses sequential regex transforms on pre-escaped HTML. Returns sanitized HTML string. Include unit tests inline or in a dedicated test.; Write failing tests for claudeStore in gui/src/__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts. Test cases: (1) initial state has sessionStatus='idle', empty messages array, currentMessageId=null; (2) handleOutboundMessage with Ready event keeps idle state; (3) sendMessage adds a user ChatMessage to messages, sets currentMessageId, calls onSend callback with message text and context; (4) handleOutboundMessage with TextDelta accumulates responseText on current assistant message, sets sessionStatus='responding'; (5) handleOutboundMessage with ThinkingDelta accumulates thinkingText, sets sessionStatus='thinking'; (6) handleOutboundMessage with ToolCall adds ToolCallInfo entry with status='pending', sets sessionStatus='tool-calling'; (7) handleOutboundMessage with ToolResult updates matching tool call status to 'complete' and stores result; (8) handleOutboundMessage with Done marks assistant message complete, sets sessionStatus='idle'; (9) handleOutboundMessage with ErrorMessage sets error on assistant message, sets sessionStatus='idle'; (10) claudeAbort calls onAbort callback, sets sessionStatus='idle'; (11) clearSession resets messages to empty array.; Implement gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts. createClaudeStore(options: {onSend, onAbort}) factory returning {state, handleOutboundMessage, sendMessage, claudeAbort, clearSession}. State uses createStore<ClaudeState>. Define and export types: SessionStatus, ToolCallInfo, AssistantMessage, UserMessage, ChatMessage, ClaudeState. handleOutboundMessage switches on msg.type and uses produce() for store mutations. sendMessage creates user message, then assistant message shell, calls onSend. Use batch() for grouped state updates.; Write failing tests for claudeStore 60fps delta batching: (1) 'buffers multiple text_delta events and flushes on rAF' — send 10 text_delta events synchronously, verify responseText is empty before rAF flush, mock requestAnimationFrame callback fires, verify responseText contains all 10 deltas concatenated; (2) 'flushes immediately on done event' — send text_delta + done, verify text is flushed without waiting for rAF; (3) 'clears pending buffer on abort'. Use vi.useFakeTimers() and manual rAF mock.; Add requestAnimationFrame-based delta batching to claudeStore: maintain a textBuffer and thinkingBuffer (plain string arrays). On text_delta/thinking_delta, push to buffer and schedule rAF if not already scheduled. rAF callback concatenates buffer contents, applies to store via setState, clears buffers. On done/error, flush buffers immediately (cancel pending rAF). On abort, clear buffers.; Write failing tests for AbortButton in gui/src/__tests__/AbortButton.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='abort-button'; (2) calls onAbort callback on click; (3) has aria-label='Abort generation'; (4) contains a stop icon element (■ character or SVG).; Implement gui/src/panels/chat/AbortButton.tsx and AbortButton.module.css. Props: {onAbort: () => void}. Simple button with ■ stop icon, themed styling matching existing button patterns. All 680 tests passing across 43 test files.

Completed all 4 remaining review-fix steps. step-19: Added 8 failing tests for markdown link protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto allowed, javascript/data/vbscript blocked). step-20: Fixed XSS by adding isSafeUrl() helper and function-based replacer in link regex — all 23 markdown tests pass. step-21: Added 4 failing tests for tool_result FIFO matching (same-tool resolution order, no re-update, unique IDs, mixed names). step-22: Fixed tool_call IDs to be locally-unique (${msg.id}-tc-${index}) and tool_result matching to filter by status='pending' — all 27 claudeStore tests pass. Full suite: 692 tests passing across 43 files. All 22 steps + 2 prerequisites now done.

Write failing tests for markdown.ts link protocol allowlist in gui/src/__tests__/markdown.test.ts. Add a new describe('link protocol validation') block with test cases: (1) 'allows http:// URLs' — renderMarkdown('[link](http://example.com)') contains ' tag. Extract a helper function `isSafeUrl(url: string): boolean` that lowercases and checks `url.startsWith('http://') || url.startsWith('https://') || url.startsWith('mailto:')`. In the regex replacer: if isSafeUrl(url) returns true, emit `${text}`; otherwise emit just the plain text (the link text without any hyperlink wrapper). Note: the URL has already been HTML-entity-escaped at this point, so the protocol check must account for that — however http/https/mailto contain no HTML-special characters, so the startsWith check works correctly on escaped input.; Write failing tests for claudeStore tool_result FIFO matching in gui/src/__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts. Add a new describe('tool_result FIFO matching') block with test cases: (1) 'resolves sequential same-tool calls in order' — send two tool_call events with the same tool_name 'reify_get_parameters' (but different tool_input), then send two tool_result events with tool_name 'reify_get_parameters' and different results. Verify the first tool_result updates toolCalls[0] to status='complete' with its result, and the second tool_result updates toolCalls[1] to status='complete' with its result; (2) 'does not re-update an already-completed tool call' — send one tool_call, one tool_result (completes it), then another tool_result with the same tool_name; verify toolCalls[0].result is unchanged (the second result does not overwrite the first); (3) 'tool calls have locally-unique IDs' — send two tool_call events with the same tool_name, verify toolCalls[0].id !== toolCalls[1].id; (4) 'mixed tool names still match correctly' — send tool_call A, tool_call B, tool_call A (same name as first), then results for B, A, A; verify each tool call is matched to its correct result in FIFO order within same-name groups.; Fix tool_result matching logic in gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts. Two changes: (1) In the tool_call handler (line 149-154), change the ToolCallInfo.id assignment from `msg.id` (the conversation message ID, same for all tool calls in a turn) to a locally-generated unique ID: `${msg.id}-tc-${m.toolCalls.length}`. This ensures each tool call has a distinct id. (2) In the tool_result handler (line 169), change `m.toolCalls.find((t) => t.toolName === msg.tool_name)` to `m.toolCalls.find((t) => t.toolName === msg.tool_name && t.status === 'pending')`. This ensures tool results are matched to the first *pending* entry with the matching tool name, so sequential same-tool calls are resolved in FIFO order rather than all updating the first entry.
